macro_segments.title: The Power of Teshuvah to Overturn Gezar Din: Rabbi Yochanan's Teaching and the Dialectic Between Decree and Repentance

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan's Opening Statement: Teshuvah Tears Up the Divine Decree
The Challenge from Rav Papa: Is Teshuvah After or Before Gezar Din?
The Healing Proof: Why the Verse Mentions Healing (Evidence of Prior Decree)

macro_segments.title: Meitivei Objection: The Baraita on Teshuvah Between Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur

micro_segments.title: The Baraita's Rule: Teshuvah Works Only Between Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur
Resolution: The Difference Between Yachid and Tzibur (Individual vs. Community)

macro_segments.title: The Case of Righteous and Wicked Israelites: How Teshuvah Operates Within the Decree (Rain Example)

micro_segments.title: Scenario One: Wicked People, Small Rain Decreed, Then They Do Teshuvah
God's Solution: Rain Arrives at the Right Time and Place (Not Overturning but Perfecting the Decree)
Scenario Two: Righteous People, Much Rain Decreed, Then They Do Teshuvah (Become Wicked)
God's Solution: Rain Falls at Wrong Times and Places (The Decree Stands but Its Effects Are Mitigated)

macro_segments.title: The Gemara's Question: Can Teshuvah Truly Tear Up Gezar Din, or Must It Work Within the System?

micro_segments.title: Gemara Challenges: Why Not Completely Tear Up the Decree Instead of Working Within It?
Response: We Work Within the System When Possible (Shani Hatam D'Efshar Behachi)

macro_segments.title: Textual Support from Jonah and Tehillim: Examples of Teshuvah After Gezar Din

micro_segments.title: Reference to Book of Jonah on Yom Kippur: God Reverses His Decree
The Pesukim from Tehillim (Psalms): Those Who Go Down to the Sea in Ships
The Special Inverted Nuns (Otiyot Penuyot) in Tehillim as Asterisks of Qualification
The Meaning of the Asterisk: Crying Out Must Be Before Gezar Din

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Akiva's Rule: Gezar Din Is Conditional on Timing (Before vs. After Gezar Din)

micro_segments.title: The Story of Bluriah HaGiyoret Challenging Rabban Gamliel: God's Favor and Divine Impartiality
Rabbi Yosei HaKohen's Parable: The Debtor, the Oath, and the King's Forgiveness
The Distinction Between Sin Against God and Sin Against Man
Rabbi Akiva's Innovation: The Two Verses Refer to Before and After Gezar Din

macro_segments.title: Gezar Din of the Yachid: Is It Reversible? Rabbi Yitzchak's Teaching on Crying Out

micro_segments.title: The Cases of Two Sick People and Two Condemned Prisoners: Same Illness, Same Judgment, Different Outcomes
Rabbi Meir's Explanation: The Difference Is in the Quality of Prayer (Tefillah Shleimah)
Rabbi Elazar's Alternative: The Difference Is in the Timing of Gezar Din (Before or After)
Rabbi Yitzchak's Resolution: Crying Out Works Both Before and After Gezar Din for the Yachid

macro_segments.title: Gezar Din of the Tzibur: Can It Be Torn Up? The Jeremiah Verses and Qualifications

micro_segments.title: The Challenge: Two Jeremiah Verses Seem to Contradict on Whether Tzibur's Sin Can Be Atoned
Resolution: Both Verses Are After Gezar Din; Difference Is Whether There Is a Shevu'ah (Oath)

macro_segments.title: Gezar Din with Shevu'ah: The House of Eli and Whether Even an Oath Can Be Circumvented

micro_segments.title: The Proof for Gezar Din with Shevu'ah: God's Oath Against the House of Eli
Rava's Loophole: The Sin Cannot Be Atoned by Sacrifice, But Can Be by Torah
Abaye's Extension: Atonement Through Torah and Acts of Kindness (Gemilas Chasadim)
Historical Application: Rabbah and Abaye as Descendants of Eli's House
The Evidence: Rabbah Lived 40 Years; Abaye (Who Also Did Gemilas Chasadim) Lived 60

macro_segments.title: The Family Descended from Eli That Was Cured Through Torah Learning

micro_segments.title: The Baraita: A Family in Jerusalem Where All Died at 18 Years Old
Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai's Diagnosis and Cure: They Are from Eli's House; Go Learn Torah
The Result: They Learned Torah and Lived; Called 'The Family of Yochanan'

macro_segments.title: Gezar Din of the Tzibur: When Is It Sealed, and Can It Be Torn Up Even After Sealing?

micro_segments.title: Rav Shmuel bar Inya's Question: The Gezar Din of a Tzibur Is Never Sealed (Lo Nechtam)

macro_segments.title: Gezar Din of the Tzibur: When Is It Sealed, and Can It Be Torn Up Even After Sealing? (continued)

micro_segments.title: Gemara's Challenge: A Verse Says 'Your Sin Is Sealed (Nechtam)' Before Me
Resolution: Even If Sealed, for a Tzibur the Gezar Din Can Be Torn Up (Nikra')
Proof from Isaiah: 'Seek the Lord When He Can Be Found' (for Tzibur, Always)

macro_segments.title: Gezar Din of the Yachid: 'Seek the Lord' Only During the Ten Days of Repentance

micro_segments.title: The Verse on Seeking God: For the Yachid, Limited to 'When He Can Be Found' (Behimmatzo)
Rabbah bar Avuha's Interpretation: The Ten Days of Repentance Between Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur
The Case of Naval: Ten-Day Reprieve to Do Teshuvah Before God's Judgment

macro_segments.title: The Judgment on Rosh Hashanah: All Humanity Passes Before God in Different Processions

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: All Those Entering the World Pass Before God Like Bnei Maron
First Interpretation: Like Sheep Being Counted for Ma'aser (Single File)
Second Interpretation (Reish Lakish): Like Ascending a Narrow Mountain Pass (Maalot Beit Maron)
Third Interpretation (Rav Yehuda): Like Soldiers of King David's Army (Chayalot Beit David)
Rabbah bar bar Chana Quoting Rabbi Yochanan: All Judged Together in One Glance
Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak: This Is Implicit in Our Mishnah's Closing Verse

macro_segments.title: The Six Times Messengers Go Out to Announce the New Moon (Rosh Chodesh)

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Messengers Go Out for Nisan (Pesach), Av (Tisha B'Av), Elul (Rosh Hashanah), Tishrei, Kislev (Chanukah), Adar (Purim)
Special Case During the Temple Period: Messengers Also Went Out for Iyar (Pesach Sheni)

macro_segments.title: The Six Times Messengers Go Out to Announce the New Moon (Rosh Chodesh) (continued)

micro_segments.title: Question: Why Not Send Out Messengers for the Minor Fasts (Tammuz, Tevet)?

macro_segments.title: The Zechariah Prophecy: Fasts Become Days of Rejoicing Depending on Circumstances

micro_segments.title: The Verse from Zechariah: The Fourth, Fifth, Seventh, and Tenth Fasts
Rav Pappa's Resolution: The Status of Fasts Depends on Peace (Shalom) and Political Freedom
Three Scenarios: Perfect Peace (Rejoicing), Government Persecution (Obligatory Fast), Middle Ground (Optional)
Why Tisha B'Av Is Different: Multiple Tragedies and the Weight of Destruction Make It Non-Optional

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Akiva's Interpretation of the Fast Days and Rabbi Shimon's Alternative Chronological Order

micro_segments.title: The Four Months in Rabbi Akiva's Reading: Tammuz (4th), Av (5th), Tishrei (7th), Teves (10th)
The Fourth Fast (Tammuz): The Breaching of Jerusalem's Wall (Shivah Asar B'Tammuz)
The Fifth Fast (Av): The Burning of the Temple (Tisha B'Av)
The Seventh Fast (Tishrei): The Assassination of Gedaliah ben Achikam (Gedaliah's Death)
The Tenth Fast (Teves): According to Rabbi Akiva, the Siege (Assarah B'Teves)
Rabbi Shimon's Alternative: The Tenth Fast Is the Fifth of Teves (Report of Destruction Reached the Exiles)
Rabbi Shimon's Logic: Perfect Chronological Order from Siege to Destruction to Assassination to Report

topical_tags.term: Ten Days of Repentance (Aseret Yemei Teshuvah)
